name: Kingdom of Prussia (1803–1892)
description: A white field bearing a black single-headed eagle (the Prussian eagle) with wings displayed, charged with the royal monogram and crowned.
history: Flag of the Kingdom of Prussia. The black eagle on white was derived from the colours of the Teutonic Order and became the heraldic identity of the House of Hohenzollern. Black-and-white were Prussia's national colours until the dissolution of the Free State of Prussia in 1947.
